1.11a: Contact via LMS portal for managed cases.
For non-LMS cases, refer to the address and telephone number on the first page of the mortgage offer.
1.11b: Contact via LMS portal for managed cases.
For non-LMS cases, refer to the contact details on the first page of the mortgage offer.
1.7: Contact via LMS portal for managed cases or details on the first page of the offer.
No contact is required for First Start mortgages if permitted under offer conditions.
1.14: Firm may act if a member of the firm or their family is the seller provided a separate fee earner of no less standing deals with the transaction and has no relation to the seller.
1.15: Firm may act if a member of the firm or their family is the borrower provided a separate fee earner of no less standing deals with the transaction and has no relation to the borrower.
3.1.3: Notify the Case Manager in writing of the seller's solicitors or licensed conveyancers firm's name and address.
3.1.4: Notification only required if there is a family or business connection between buyer and seller involving a gift or undervalue sale.
Use LMS portal or contact details on the first page of the offer.
3.1.5: No other identity verification documents are acceptable.
3.2.3: Notify the Case Manager in writing of the seller's legal representative's name and address.
3.2.4: Contact is only necessary for family/business connections involving gifts or undervalue sales.
Use LMS portal or contact details on the offer.
4.1: Lender does not provide a copy of the valuation report to the conveyancer.
Conveyancers should use information in the mortgage offer for required property checks.
4.5b: Submit Certificate of Title via LMS portal for managed cases.
For non-LMS cases, send to the contact details on the front page of the offer.
5.4.4: Only send environmental or contaminated land reports if prompted by entries in the Local Authority search.
5.4.5: Personal searches must be performed by firms registered under The Search Code and monitored by the Property Codes Compliance Board.
5.4.6: For remortgages up to £1 million: No Local Authority search or search insurance required; lender procures own indemnity policy.
For remortgages up to £1 million: Mining, Cheshire Brine, or commons registration searches are not mandatory but recommended.
For purchases: Search insurance is accepted provided the conveyancer provides an unqualified certificate of title.
5.5.3b: Original or copy of planning permission is not required.
5.5.3c: Original or copy of building regulation consents is not required.
5.5.3d: Certificates of lawful use or development are not required.
5.7.1a: Lending on flying freeholds is permitted if it only affects part of the property.
Conveyancer must report the extent and specific accommodation within the flying freehold.
5.7.1b: Lending on freehold flats is not permitted.
5.8.1: A first charge is required on the freehold title if it is wholly owned by the borrower, even if the primary security is leasehold.
5.8.5: Lender requires a first charge on both leasehold and freehold interests for reciprocal leasehold/freehold arrangements.
5.13.1: Report if borrower is not providing the balance of purchase price or proposes a second charge via 1.11 contact points.
5.14.1: Unexpired lease term must be at least the mortgage term plus 45 years.
5.14.9: Initial ground rent must not exceed 0.1% of property value or £500 per annum, whichever is higher.
Ground rent escalation linked to RPI or similar index is acceptable without referral.
Multipliers that double every 5, 10, or 15 years are considered unreasonable and must be referred to the lender.
Leases must be amended if they do not comply with these ground rent criteria.
5.14.11: Indemnity insurance is accepted for lease defects where a Deed of Variation is not possible, subject to standard Part 1 requirements.
5.14.12: Ground rent/service charge receipts should not be sent to the lender but must be obtained by the conveyancer before completion.
5.14.13: Receipted copy of notice or evidence of service must be kept with the title deeds after completion.
5.14.15b: Indemnity insurance for absentee/insolvent landlords is only accepted if it covers failure to insure or repair common parts and services.
5.14.17b: Conveyancer must confirm building safety documentation complies with the Building Safety Act 2022.
5.14.17c: Conveyancer must confirm building safety documentation complies with the Building Safety Act 2022 for remortgages.
5.15.2b: Management company share certificate must be kept with the title deeds after completion.
5.15.2c: Signed blank stock transfer form must be kept with the title deeds after completion.
5.15.2d: Management company memorandum and articles of association must be kept with the title deeds after completion.
5.17.5a: Power of Attorney documentation must be kept with the title deeds after completion.
5.17.5b: Statutory declaration of non-revocation of power of attorney must be kept with the title deeds after completion.
5.19.1: Shared ownership lending is not permitted.
Affordable housing and shared equity schemes require referral for consideration.
5.20.1: Report if the lease does not meet UK Finance minimum requirements for solar PV panel roof space leases.
5.20.4: Disclose details of any existing Green Deal Plans.
6.4.4: Disclosure of Incentives Form is required.
Subsequent changes to incentives must be reported unless they fall under the 9 July 2020 stamp duty incentive exception.
Exceptions for pre-July 2020 apps: Cash instead of stamp duty is allowed if ≤5% of purchase price and ≤original stamp duty incentive value.
6.6.3: Counterpart or certified copy of the tenancy agreement must be kept with the title deeds after completion.
6.6.4: Lending is prohibited if: 5+ adults occupy, multiple tenancy agreements exist, or mandatory/additional licensing applies.
6.7.1: Acceptable schemes: NHBC, Zurich Municipal, LABC, Build-Zone, Premier Guarantee (cover ≥ market value/insurance figure), Building Life Plan (with contaminated land/health & safety cover), Castle 10 from Checkmate (including outbuildings), The Q Policy, CRL, ICW, Protek, Advantage HCI, Aedis Warranties Limited, Global Home Warranty.
6.7.3: Building standards indemnity scheme assignments must be kept with the title deeds after completion.
6.7.4: Professional Consultants Certificate is required if no new home warranty is available.
6.7.6: Professional consultant's certificate must be kept with the title deeds after completion.
6.10.2: The lender no longer makes retentions from mortgage advances.
7.3: Consent from all occupants aged 17+ is required, except for Buy to Let offers with assured shorthold tenants.
8.1: Conveyancer may not advise the borrower if they have a First Start mortgage offer unless special conditions are met.
May not advise any other specified third parties except those in bullet point 3.
9.1: Indemnity insurance policies must be kept with the title deeds after completion.
9.2: Indemnity insurance limit must be at least the market value of the property.
10.2a: Advance is paid electronically; a £15 fee is deducted.
10.2b: Minimum 3 working days notice required for the advance.
Additional 5 working days notice required if a final inspection is needed.
10.7: Delayed completion: Advance must be returned via telegraphic transfer within 1 working day.
Return to the bank account on page 2 of the Certificate of Title quoting the mortgage account number.
10.11: The lender may charge interest at the mortgage rate if the return of the advance is delayed.
14.1.4: Form CH2 required only for stage releases, retentions, deferred interest, or instalment loans.
14.2.1: LMS managed: registration documents/delays managed via LMS system.
Non-LMS cases: Send to Mortgage Services Department, Securities Section, Bank of Ireland UK, PO Box 3191, One Temple Quay, Bristol, BS1 9HY (DX 98850 Bristol 2).
14.2.2: Documents to upload for LMS: Priority Notice (OS1/OS2/K17), Registration confirmation (AP1/receipt), Official Copy of Register.
Delays over 30 days must be reported to LMS every 30 days.

Documents to keep/send: Professional Consultant’s Certificate, Indemnity policy, Power of Attorney, First Start Mortgage Deed, BTL Tenancy Agreement, Receipted Notice of Transfer/Mortgage, Management Company documentation.
16.1.1: Contact Mortgage Services Department, Bristol: 0345 602 7056 or DX 98850 Bristol 2.
16.3.7b: Attestation clause: 'THE COMMON SEAL OF BANK OF IRELAND (UK) PLC was affixed hereto by authority of the Directors:'
16.4.1: Contact for consent to let: Mortgage Services Department (0345 602 7056).
Buy to Let mortgages: refer to offer special conditions.
16.4.2: Proposed tenancy agreement must be sent to the lender, unless it is a Buy to Let complying with offer conditions.
17.2.1b: Discharge is handled directly with the Land Registry.
